title: Boston-area restaurant chain takes Storrowing as opportunity to apologize, and plug their pizza
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Sal's Pizza truck gets stuck on Storrow Drive, owner issues public apology on Facebook.
The family-owned chain, Sal's Pizza, used the incident to promote their jumbo pizza slices.
Sal's Pizza was founded in 1990 and has over 100 locations in New England and Florida.

title: As heat wave looms, more beaches close due to sewage spill
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Beaches along the North Shore, including Crane and Plum Island, are closed due to a sewage spill in Haverhill.
Approximately 8 million gallons of untreated wastewater enter the Merrimack River daily after sewer main breaks in Haverhill.
Authorities work to divert wastewater as the holiday weekend nears, raising health and economic concerns in the region.

title: Condom maker relocates headquarters to historic Lynn dairy creamery
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Global Protection Corp relocates headquarters from Boston Seaport to historic Lynn creamery at 630 Lynnway.
The move establishes Lynn as the nation's leading hub for condom shipments, handling 250 million annually.
Global Protection Corp's new 65,000-square-foot space accommodates manufacturing, fulfillment, and 60 employees.

title: Supreme Court TPS decision leaves Haitian community, advocates reeling in Mass.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: The Supreme Court ruled 6-3 to end Temporary Protected Status for Haitian and Syrian immigrants, impacting 356,000 people nationwide.
Massachusetts, home to 45,000 TPS holders, braces for community and economic impacts, particularly in health care and education sectors.
Massachusetts officials are urging Congress to provide permanent legal protections for TPS holders, denouncing the ruling's potential consequences.

title: In San Francisco, even $180,000 tech salaries are no longer enough
author: New York Times Service
bullets: Tech workers in San Francisco struggle with living costs, even with salaries over $180,000 due to rising rents.
The city's cost of living index shows expenses 65.6% above the national average, impacting housing affordability.
AI companies like OpenAI and Anthropic are creating new elites, further straining resources for other tech workers.

title: Inspired by its anti-Belichick coach, Paraguay forged a famous win in Foxborough
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Paraguay defeated Germany in a nail-biting penalty shootout in Foxborough, advancing to the World Cup Round of 16.
Coach Gustavo Alfaro led Paraguay to the unexpected victory, marking the nation's greatest World Cup win in history.
Germany missed three penalties in the shootout, an unprecedented occurrence in their World Cup history.

title: Jaylen Brown has reportedly ‘grown frustrated’ with Celtics amid offseason rife with trade rumors
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown is reportedly frustrated with the Celtics due to persistent trade rumors linking him to other teams.
The Celtics have continued to receive trade inquiries for Brown despite his significant contributions to last season's 56-win campaign.
Celtics President Brad Stevens remains noncommittal about Brown's future but stresses his importance to the team.

title: Red Sox’ Willson Contreras sheds tears, gets ejected in emotional night at Fenway Park
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox first baseman Willson Contreras hit a three-run homer in a 6-3 win against the Nationals.
Contreras shed tears in the dugout over recent earthquakes in Venezuela, affecting his emotions during the game.
Contreras was ejected after challenging an umpire's ruling on his strikeout, deemed disrespectful by the umpire.

title: Are these Boston-area colleges really $100K a year? It’s complicated.
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Published costs at some Boston-area colleges exceed $100,000 annually, but financial aid significantly reduces the actual cost for many students.
A significant portion of students receive institutional grants or aid, lowering the overall amount they pay at private colleges and universities.
Colleges use tuition from wealthier families to subsidize costs for lower-income students, offering merit scholarships and grants to aid affordability.

title: 2 Ariana Grande concerts at TD Garden are rescheduled
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Ariana Grande's TD Garden concerts on July 22 and 24 rescheduled to July 23 and 26 respectively.
The rescheduling is due to production challenges, ensuring full and safe delivery of the concerts.
All tickets remain valid for the new dates, and the July 25 show will proceed as originally scheduled.
